question_text: Explain why adding more resistors in parallel decreases the total resistance of a circuit, whereas adding more resistors in series increases it.
correct_answer: In series, current must flow through every resistor one after another, so each resistor adds to the total opposition. In parallel, current has multiple paths to flow through, so adding more branches gives the current more routes, reducing total opposition.
explanation: This tests understanding of how current behaves differently in series and parallel circuits. In series, resistances add because current is forced through all of them. In parallel, additional paths allow current to bypass some resistors, so total resistance decreases.
